description_long: This object is a monolithic stone figure resembling the iconic Moai statues of Easter Island, characterized by a prominent elongated face, deep-set eyes, a broad nose, and a pronounced jawline. The torso is blocky and stylized, with subtle indications of arms resting along the sides and a slightly protruding chest. The back of the figure is relatively flat with some surface irregularities and faint circular markings, possibly representing weathering or symbolic carvings. The stone surface appears rough and weathered, with a matte, dark gray coloration and occasional lighter patches. The overall form is massive and imposing, suggesting it serves as a robust barrier or marker in an urban or park setting. Its solid construction and lack of moving parts indicate it is designed to be a fixed, immovable obstacle.
description: A large, rough-hewn stone figure with a stylized human face and torso, reminiscent of a Moai statue, featuring a dark, weathered surface and blocky proportions.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full face, broad nose, deep-set eyes, and blocky chest.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated profile, prominent nose, and angular jawline.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals a flat back with surface irregularities and faint circular markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the profile of the face and the arm contours along the torso.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to parks, plazas, or historical sites, or as a decorative barrier along urban sidewalks or near pedestrian crossings.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground and upright, often partially embedded in the pavement or soil for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible surface erosion and smoothing, especially on protruding facial features and edges, likely caused by prolonged exposure to weather, occasional contact with pedestrians, and possible vehicle brushing. Some discoloration and minor chipping are present on the lower edges.
